title: thymus picentinus
intro: Thymus picentinus, also known as Picentia thyme, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to the Italian region of Campania, where it grows in the rocky, dry areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, respiratory problems, and digestive issues. Thymus picentinus can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and it is also used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation, improve sleep, and boost the immune system.
